refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the load→travel→dump cycle.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR specifies a single functional obligation: to support achieving a defined cycle time target for a specific operational cycle. It is already focused on what the system must do (support achieving the cycle time target) without specifying how, thus it is solution-neutral and atomic.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the cycle time for the load→travel→dump cycle under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions, and verify that the 95th percentile (p95) cycle time is less than or equal to the customer-defined target (CT_target).
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the load→travel→dump cycle.

candidate_dps: Optimize load mechanism speed and efficiency
Optimize dump mechanism speed and efficiency
Coordinate load, travel, and dump operations to minimize idle time
Implement adaptive cycle time control based on real-time conditions
selected_dp.selected_dp: Coordinate load, travel, and dump operations to minimize idle time

potential_sources: Interdependence between load, travel, and dump timing
coupling_check.notes: Selecting coordination as the DP explicitly addresses the interaction between sub-operations, aiming to minimize coupling by managing dependencies through scheduling rather than tightly coupling physical mechanisms.

assumptions_risks: Assumes that load, travel, and dump mechanisms can be controlled independently to some extent
Risk that real-time coordination may be limited by sensor or control system latency
Assumes site conditions and duty cycle remain within expected ranges for cycle time optimization
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider refining the DP to explicitly separate control logic from physical mechanism optimization to further reduce coupling and improve modularity.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the overall coordination logic for load, travel, and dump operations, integrating task scheduling and resource management to minimize idle time across vehicles.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time status updates from vehicles and sends coordination commands.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Sends task assignments and operation schedules to vehicles.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Facilitates communication between NGHE Solution and vehicle modules.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as coordination logic naturally belongs here; minimal diffusion since it interfaces with vehicles via defined communication channels.
Vehicles execute tasks but do not coordinate multiple vehicles; placing coordination here would cause high diffusion and coupling.
Teleoperation focuses on remote control, not coordination; poor containment and high diffusion.
Safety Services handle alerts and safety logic, unrelated to operational coordination; very poor containment.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for containing the coordination DP because it centralizes scheduling and task management, minimizing diffusion and coupling with other modules.
containment_risks: Potential complexity growth in NGHE Solution as coordination logic expands.
Dependency on reliable communication interfaces may cause delays affecting coordination effectiveness.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP logically addresses the FR by focusing on coordinating load, travel, and dump operations to minimize idle time, which directly supports achieving the customer-defined cycle time target.

diffusion_risks: Low diffusion risk as coordination logic is centralized within the NGHE Solution module, minimizing scattering across multiple modules.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high containment (score 9) and low diffusion (score 2), indicating appropriate architectural packaging of the DP. Other modules have poor containment and high diffusion scores, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the implementation boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through measuring the cycle time p95 against the CT_target under agreed duty cycle and site conditions, as well as ensuring compliance with worksite safety plans and operational policies.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution module is clear and well-justified, with defined interfaces for telemetry, tasking, and network communication supporting the coordination function.

recommended_revisions: Monitor complexity growth within the NGHE Solution module to manage maintainability as coordination logic expands.
Ensure robust and reliable communication interfaces to mitigate risks of delays affecting coordination effectiveness.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with strong justification and low architectural risk. The DP logically addresses the FR, the DP is well-contained within a single implementation module minimizing diffusion risks, and verification feasibility is confirmed with clear allocation. Given the high safety criticality, it is prudent to avoid unnecessary complexity by stopping decomposition here and focusing on monitoring and managing identified risks.

revise.why: Monitor complexity growth within the NGHE Solution module and ensure robust communication interfaces to maintain maintainability and coordination effectiveness.

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module will maintain its containment and low diffusion as coordination logic expands.
Verification methods for cycle time and safety compliance are reliable and sufficient.
open_risks: Potential complexity growth within the NGHE Solution module affecting maintainability.
evidence_needed: Ongoing monitoring data on module complexity and maintainability.
Performance metrics on communication reliability and coordination effectiveness.
